metaTitle: Join the Founder waitlist · Cycling Zone
heroHeadline: Build your cycling team. Race the world. Back a fair manager game.
heroSub: Cycling Zone is a browser-based cycling manager where tactics, long-term planning and community rivalry matter. I'm exploring premium, but the rule is simple:
heroSubStrong: the game must be fair for everyone. You cannot pay for better riders, faster training, or better results.
promiseBody: Premium can unlock identity, convenience, analytics and ways to back development. It cannot unlock better race results, faster training, stronger riders, transfer advantages, improved scouting odds or hidden power.
promiseFootnote: This promise appears here and near any future pricing info. It's the most important trust message in the whole premium conversation.
tiersTitle: What might fair premium look like?
tiersSub: Decisions made 2026-05-14. The survey tests different price points, so Premium varies between 29/49/69 DKK depending on which link you clicked.
founderNoteTitle: Where does the Founder waitlist fit?
founderNoteBody: Founder is not a separate paid tier. It's an early waitlist status for the first 100 players who want to be considered if payment opens later. Joining is non-binding, and you are charged nothing today.

tierFree.name: Free Manager
bullets: Nothing cut from the core game

fairnessTitle: What may be sold, and what must never be sold
fairnessSub: The brand promise as concrete rules. Rock-solid.
notSold: Exclusive ranked races for paying users
benefitsTitle: What do Founder waitlist members get?
benefitsBody: Founder status is identity and a thank-you, never competitive advantage. The final list is decided together with the community before payment opens.

benefitsList.title: Permanent founder badge
benefitsList.desc: Identity mark that can't be bought later.

benefitsList.title: Vote on non-balance roadmap topics
benefitsList.desc: Never about balance or competitive advantage.

formSectionSub: It's NOT binding. You are charged nothing today. The goal is to hear whether enough cyclists want to back the project financially before I build payment.

faqItems.a: No. The competitive core must stay fair. Premium is about identity, convenience, analytics and ways to back development.
Yes. Free players have full access to the competitive core game. That's the foundation of fairness.

faqItems.q: Can premium users buy stronger riders or faster training?
faqItems.a: No. Those features must not be sold. Period.
To hear whether Cycling Zone can become sustainable before I build the wrong thing. Better to learn before investing.
No. Waitlist first, payment later, and only if the community response is positive. You're charged nothing when joining.

faqItems.q: What happens if people don't want premium?
faqItems.a: Then premium waits, and focus returns to retention, gameplay and community growth. That's the whole point of asking first.

form.contactHelp: At least one of the fields below must be filled.
form.tierHelp: I'm testing different price points, your answer is not binding.
form.mainReasonLabel: What's the main reason you're considering backing the project? (optional)
form.fairnessLabel: Anything that would make you say no? (optional)
form.followUpConsent: It's OK to contact me with up to 2 follow-up questions (interview / short survey). This is optional.
form.gdprBefore: I have read and accept the
form.gdprAfter: and consent to storing my email + Discord handle for the purpose stated above.
form.submitDisclaimer: We don't spam and never share your info. You can always email us to be removed.
form.successBody: Thanks for joining early. We'll email when premium opens, and before that if we have questions about what you think.

interestOptions.unsure: Unsure, want to know more first

supporter_monthly.sub: 49 DKK/mo, back the project and unlock founder badge
